3.title: "Onimaru is unpredictable." No one expects an Inspire Saber. Swap the Piercer for Malefic Roar or Sea Halberd if your teammates are vegetables, or do it late game by selling your boots instead. Prioritize squishies, of course, and turret pushing. You need Bargain Hunter for expensive items and Weakness Finder to ensure they won't escape you (unless they have multiple blinks or dashes, so use this strategically). If the squishies are close to each other and you think you won't be stunned, you can use S1 + Inspire to, as Saber puts it, "KILL ALL AT ONCE." Or just use S1 + Inspire to rush turrets.

12.title:
Haas Claws - Lifesteal
Demon Boots - Mana Regen
Berseker Fury - Crit Damage
Malefic Gun - Attack Range
Windtalker - Attack Speed
Wind of Nature - Immunity

Tips:
• Always buy Haas Claws first to have lifesteal in early game.
• In late game, replace the Demon Boots for Warrior/Tough Boots for physical attack and movement speed.
• When choosing battle spells: Aegis, for protection against tanks and enemy attacks, if you know how to use Bruno properly when it comes to dashing and escaping, use this spell, but it gets useless in late game because the enemies now have full items in late game, the shield breaks easily so be careful when using Aegis. Flicker, this is a suitable battle spell for Bruno and beginners should always start on Flicker as their main battle spell, to escape against enemies. Purify, to cleanse debuffs and makes you immune to crowd control for 1.2s and gives you movement speed.
• When choosing battle emblems: Fatal (1st Tier), gains 4% of critical damage against enemies. Tenacity (2nd Tier), it increases your damage reduction when your HP is low. Quantum Charge (3rd Tier), it provides HP regeneration and a burst of movement speed whenever you deal damage with a basic attack.

25.title:
optimized kagura build after nerf

what can be changed?
genius wand for one shots early game (lvl 6 - 7)
glowing wand can be changed with sky piercer, recommend glowing wand for heavy life stealers or healers (ruby, thaumuz..)
lightning truncheon can be changed with wishing lantern if enemies are tanky and can sustain lots of damage
divine glaive can be changed with other items ex: blood wings, queens wings etc
magic crystal shouldn't be changed AT ALL always keep as your 5th or last item.

emblems
inspire for a total of 10% cd on emblem, 20% cd in total with truncheon or wishing lantern
weapon master for extra damage 
if you want to maximize her damage further with emblems, switch inspire with rupture for adaptive penetration and/or war cry for 8% more damage, impure rage for extra mana and damage to tanks
use lethal ignition for finishers, very good when paired up with glowing wand

battle spells
flicker, and flameshot are all good items for kagura
petrify is also very underated

all done!

30.title:
This build synergizes around yi sun shin's passive Heavenly Vow, which switches between melee (crit + splash damage) and ranged (safe poke) every basic attack. Endless Battle for true damage after each skill, so when you weave skill basic  switch stance, you’re constantly triggering bonus damage. Hunter Strike gives movement speed after hits, letting you reposition fast to keep that passive cycle going. Blade of Despair and Malefic Roar scale your burst hard, especially when enemies are low or tanky. Assassin emblem with Killing Spree adds sustain after kills, so you dive, reset, and snowball. Overall, it works because you’re basically abusing constant stance-switching + true damage procs to output high DPS while staying mobile and hard to punish.
You could also go for sky piercer if you snowball early
Try to play safe and farm as much as you can but you should also assist your team while your at it 
It's easy to reach hit lvl 15 on yss before the 10 minute mark if you are consistent 
Hope this helps :)

Consider Oracle your Car insurance, it buffs passive shield allowing you to almost have an extra life, use sprint to run away

If passive is on cooldown then you probably won't need oracle, here are some adjustments

Change warrior boots to magic boots to remain at 40% cooldown reduction

Switch oracle to athena's shield if you need magic damage reduction or antique cuirass if you need physical damage reduction

Place both defense items in your spare item slots for faster access

And yes, the car insurance will save you and is worth it

33.title:
This build works really well for Cyclops because you don’t need to stack a lot of damage items on him. What he really needs is cooldown reduction so he can keep spamming his skills, along with enough sustain to stay in fights longer.

This setup helps you stay in battle consistently and is also effective if you want to use Cyclops in the jungle. It performs well against burst heroes since you can outlast their damage, and it’s also reliable against sustain heroes because of your constant skill pressure.

Overall, it focuses more on uptime and survivability rather than pure burst, which fits Cyclops’ playstyle really well.

55.author: ferryferrariel

55.title:
Battle Medic. Carry Yourself to Solo queue Mythic.
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
Skill Unlock Order
 1>2>1>3. Then focus leveling first ability whenever possible.
Buy in build order, skip sky piercer for late game if you don't get kills within first 8 minutes or the team has less kills than the enemy. 
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
Play Style:
Hyper Aggressive. As soon as you spawn, unlock the first ability to get more stacks before you reach mid. Then bombard the mage or roam with your first, make sure your passive stacks on them. Stick to MM for the first 5 minutes or till you push that lane. You have the simple job of making sure your enemies hate your guts in every team fight and every chase. 
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
Item Description:

Demon Shoes: The only reason you're getting this is to solve the mana problem. You'll be very mana hungry early game but also need slot for damage items instead of mana items.

Fleeting Time: I dont know what Moontoon was smoking when they buffed her ult, with his item, you can have an ult uptime of 100%. A single or even double kill\assist during your ult ensures that you get to ult AGAIN as soon as you finish your ult. Buy this asap.

Sky Piercer: Snowball item, since your enemy is busy buying anti heal, you're building damage to finish them off, if you manage to get kills during your ult within the first 10 minutes of the match, buy this and steal kills with your first ability. I've gone upwards of 24 kills in a single match.

Lightning Truncheon, Holy Crystal, Genius Wand: Damage items with good scaling, i've tested all items, these work the best in all scenarios.
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
Skill Checks:
As Angela, You can play the entire game without even looking at your screen and just focusing on the map. Do that. Unless enemy has natalia or aamon, you have no reason to never look at the map. Learn to move while only focusing on the map. 

You can hold your ult key and hover over teammates to see what they are up to. Note that they can also tell that you're looking at them. Use this to encourage your exp or hyper to dive for a kill. Remember, YOU need those kills. Unless you're in mythic, YOU deserve and NEED those kills. 

In case you are lacking kills or the team is losing, skip the sky piercer and buy the flask to get more sustain. Don't worry about heals, your healing also scales with your magic power. The same power that is used to kill the enemies is used to heal. 

DO. NOT. RECALL. Unless you manage to run out of mana, just use your first ability to heal and keep your teammates in the lane, or ult and rotate to help the other lane push. You're a battle medic. Its your JOB to keep them in the fight. Throw a slur if you have to, keep them in the fight.

Use your first ability to damage enemies and heal allies. Try to focus on the damage aspect, In a chase, this is what will save you, the more it hits, the slower the enemies become. 

Flameshot serves two purposes. Spacing/escape and sniping/kills. Remember, you get sky piercer stacks even from a flameshot kills. You can use it even during your ult. Using it in close range trades damage for knockback, if a melee character is too close like argus, aldous or any tank. Just use flame shot, works especially well against tigreals. Spam it whenver he tries to combo, it will ruin it. Plus, the knock back is aoe. So you can throw away 5 enemies at the same time if they gank you.
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

Check out my other builds for other play styles.

59.title:
The common emblem is taken for HP and mana regeneration. With it, you don't need an Enchanted Talisman, and you can go full tank and be immediately useful, plus more confident laning.

Agility for extra speed and the ability to kite opponents.

Tenacity and Brave Smite for greater survivability; take Brave Smite if you're having a tough game and you're even more determined to survive.

We take Impure Rage if we're confident and want to poke in lane, plus it's easier to mount and kill cores.

You can also play with support emblems and an Enchanted Talisman, but that's complete nonsense, and no one's played it for a long time.

First, buy full Physical/Magic Defense Boots (check the enemy pick) to tank more on the first turtle and rally to the team faster thanks to movement speed. Then buy Oracle to increase your passive healing and skill cooldowns. Then, focusing on the enemy pick, choose between two items: Anti-Heal (Dominance Ice) and Chastise Pauldron (reduces enemy attack speed + additional healing). If your team doesn't have Anti-Heal, buy Dominance; if they already have it, buy Pauldron.

Next, check the enemy pick to see what you need: Magic Defense or Physical Defense.

If you're playing against sustained magical damage, buy Radiant Armor; against instant magical damage, buy Athena's Shield. If you're facing a lot of physical damage, consider buying an Antique Cuirass, but if you're playing against marksmen who often use their basic attack, consider a Blade Armor. The final slots are up to you, whether it's Immortality or a Guardian Helmet.

62.title: This Kaja roam build is focused on mobility, utility, and durability. Rapid Boots help with faster rotations and ganks, while Thunder Belt provides sustain, slows, and extra damage during fights. Antiheal for countering lifesteal and regeneration heroes, and defensive items like Antique Cuirass, Athena’s Shield(radiant armour depending on the enemy's draft)or Oracle are chosen depending on the enemy lineup and match situation. If the team is snowballing, Fleeting Time can also be added for more frequent ultimate usage and pick-offs. Immortality is used more as a situational or late game side item for extra survivability during crucial team fights. Overall, the build is designed to support teammates, control engagements, and secure key targets consistently.
62.author: Creepylookingthirsty

62.title: Mid Kaja, faster rotation and strong early team fight. Strong damage if have 3 items, can 1 hit combo soft mage/mm heroes. Use ult, 2nd skill, 1st skill, then hit with passive. In team fight, just first skill and hit with passive, using your fast movement speed to poke, get good position, and scout enemies.
62.author: LordgrimZ

62.title:
This build focuses on high regeneration to compensate for Kaja’s relatively low survivability. Since Kaja needs to stay in the middle of fights without strong crowd control immunity, he relies heavily on self-healing from his first skill. The selected items are designed to support his survivability. Clock of Destiny and Oracle have strong synergy, improving both his durability and healing effectiveness. Thunderbelt is a great early investment, allowing Kaja to remain impactful even into the late game. 

Additionally, Conceal Boots further enhance Kaja’s playstyle by taking advantage of the roaming system, allowing him to gain gold more efficiently. By constantly poking enemies and rotating through the jungle and river, Kaja can create ambush opportunities. His first skill and passive make him very effective in this role, enabling him to maintain a high gold income (GPM) when played correctly. The goal is to reach 40% cooldown reduction so Kaja can spam his first skill more frequently. This helps him sustain through healing while also applying his passive debuff, which reduces the enemy’s damage output—making him tankier in a unique way. 

Since Kaja deals magic damage, Glowing Wand is a strong choice. It provides bonus movement speed, applies burn damage, and helps slow enemies, making it easier for Kaja to survive while staying close to his targets. The combination of Tough Boots and Brute Force Breastplate helps Kaja handle heavy crowd control. The additional movement speed from Brute Force also allows him to stay within range and maintain pressure on enemies. For emblems, the Support emblem is the best option due to its synergy with his first skill. Because his healing is based on a flat value, achieving 40% cooldown reduction is crucial to maximize its effectiveness during fights. The Brave Smite talent further enhances his sustain, as frequent skill usage ensures consistent healing in short intervals. 

As a result, Kaja gains significant bonus movement speed from Thunderbelt, Glowing Wand, Brute Force Breastplate, and Support emblems. This high mobility allows him to stay on top of enemies, while continuously slowing them through his first skill, passive, and Thunderbelt effects. The combination makes it difficult for enemies to escape, while also improving Kaja’s survivability in extended fights. 

Note: Kaja’s passive can also trigger Brave Smite, making it effective in almost any situation for additional healing.

74.title:
Decent hybrid build, Domiance ice can be swapped with sea halberd against heroes like lapu, otherwise malefic gun for pen. I prefer malefic gun over malefic roar for the extra basic attack distance it provides for minsis second skill but It doesnt really matter tbh. Corrosion scythe is a must first, dhs for extra sustain especially against tankier exp laners. Guardian helmet is a comfort pick for the synergy with minsis passive, whilst dominance ice is a general defense item. you can either do one defense item yourself depending on the enemy lineup, or just have guardian helmet.

Emblems - straight forward. Fighter emblem safest, marksmen most scale heavy, assassin high risk high reward. Minsis playstyle is a setter, disabler, and attack speed. crit build minsi just isnt it. as for pyst, minsis best, most unpredicatbe comboes revolve around flicker. its nice to have it available more often, otherwise master assassin is recc imo

94.title:
Hunting Hound Kupa

This build focuses on strengthening kupa

Dominance ice passive applies so he's a good tank with anti heal, blade of despair increases his hp and corrosion scythe applies full slow value since his bite isn't a ranged attack
Lastly, focusing mark increases his damage

Popol and kupa is probably the only duo in the game who can apply 1.5× the slow of corrosion scythe gaining more than full value

I know there isn't much attack speed and since I'm focusing on kupa, I added cooldown reduction to allow ult spam increasing movement speed to chase while healing kupa

Downside is pretty obvious, compared to other builds this one lacks lifesteal, there's no fix for this sadly, I added fighter emblem and called it a day, it's somewhat effective with the power potion

95.title:
This build isn’t for every type of enemies. This is a general build, not for chase nor soaking a lot of damage.

40% CDR, 
High regen capabilities from War Axe, Queen’s Wings, and Oracle.
20 (Oracle) + 50 (RGM) + 8 (Fighter Emblem) Hybrid Defense.
High “low HP clutch” potential (Queen’s Wings’ damage reduction + RGM’s shield + Oracle boosts shield and spell vamp).
Medium Extra HP > Higher chance of activating 8% damage bonus from Sea Halberd.
Above average att spd > Faster 2nd dash from 3rd skill.
High Physical att + dmg bonus from War Cry, Sea Halberd, and Queen’s Wings.

When possible, replace Magic Shoes with BFB. 10% CDR, 0-48 adaptive att, 25% resilience, and extra mov spd.
If you need to replace an item to buy a counter item, discard Oracle/RGM/BFB.

To those who think this build contains too much of damage items, just know this.
 HP Recovered (Spell Vamp) = Skill Damage x Spell Vamp x Spell Vamp Ratio.

By the time I’m writing this, I’ve achieved 4334 matches with 60% WR on YZ. Expect this build won’t be effective as time goes on, due to game changes (emblems, items, META heroes, etc.)

101.title:
I,It balances high-velocity early wave clear with devastating late-game magic penetration and durability to protect your Hero Power.⚔️ Equipment Purchase Order
  1,Arcane Boots: Gives early Magic Penetration to ensure your Skill 1 pokes chunk enemy health lines immediately.
  2,Ice Queen Wand: Your absolute core item. Grants the crucial "sticky" slow effect on every skill hit, locking enemies in place.
  3,Clock of Destiny: Provides a massive pool of HP and Mana. (Pro-tip: Rush the Elegant Gem component piece early for free lane   sustain).
  4,Divine Glaive: Destroys mid-game frontline tanks by instantly slicing through early Magic Defense items like Athena's Shield.
  5,Blood Wings: Adds raw endgame Magic Power alongside a massive protective shield and movement speed boost to survive late-game assassin dives.
  6,Holy Crystal: The ultimate finisher. Its percentage-based passive multiplies the huge Magic Power pool built from your previous  items, maximizing your end-game burst.
II,Emblem Set
     Main Emblem: Mage (For flat Magic Power, Cooldown Reduction, and Magic Penetration).
         Ability 1: Rupture (+5 Adaptive Penetration to secure early-game lane dominance).
         Ability 2: Pull Yourself Together (Reduces your Battle Spell and item cooldowns by 15%).
         Ability 3: Impure Rage (Deals extra adaptive damage based on enemy HP and restores Mana continuously).
III,Battle SpellFlicker: Mandatory for instantaneous repositioning, escaping ganks, or adjusting your location during your Ultimate. IV,Situational Swaps (The Counter Rules)
 (i)Against Heavy Regen (Estes, Angela, Uranus, Terizla): Replace Blood Wings with Glowing Wand in your 4th slot to slash enemy healing by 50%.
 (ii)Against High-Mobility Burst (Fanny, Ling, Saber): Swap Flicker to Purify or Petrify
101.author: Nyx_Ast

102.title: This build focuses on Mathilda’s ability to go in and out freely while constantly annoying enemies. By combining Thunderbelt with her passive, she can keep enemies slowed and pressured, making it very difficult for them to catch or punish her. The use of Queen’s Wings may seem unusual at first, but it serves as an emergency tool. When her HP drops significantly, the damage reduction effect gives her enough time to escape or reset the fight. At the same time, Flask of the Oasis provides additional support by helping reduce cooldowns and improving her overall utility for the team. The main goal of this build is to allow Mathilda to engage, poke, and disengage safely. She can jump in, apply pressure, provide vision, and then get out without being punished. This makes her extremely hard to deal with, especially in prolonged fights. With her high mobility, Mathilda can also threaten the enemy backline, targeting mages, assassins, or marksmen. She doesn’t always need to secure kills—simply disrupting their positioning and forcing them to play defensively is already a big advantage for her team. You have two spell options: Sprint and Purify. The choice depends on the enemy lineup. If the enemy relies heavily on slows, Sprint is the better option since it helps you maintain mobility and keep your in–out playstyle smooth. However, if the enemy has strong single-target or long-duration crowd control, Purify becomes more valuable to prevent getting locked down. Always remember that Mathilda’s role in this build is to constantly annoy enemies. You want to keep poking them with your passive’s bonus damage while applying slow from Thunderbelt. At the same time, you gain significant movement speed from your passive and talents, allowing you to stay elusive and difficult to catch while maintaining pressure.

111.title:
Cara memberikan damage:
Starlium Scythe : Saya lebih suka menggunakan ini ketimbang Feather of Heaven (di season ini), karena ada banyak tank yang menggunakan Chastise Pauldron sehinga meng-counter Attack Speed-nya; Damage Starlium Scythe lebih konsisten karena memiliki cooldown konstan dan bersifat True Damage sehingga tidak bisa di-counter oleh item Defense. Selain itu, Starlium Scythe juga memberikan efek slow yang tidak dimiliki Feather of Heaven.

Cara sustain:
Pada early game, Edith biasanya memberikan dps yang lebih besar dengan menggunakan skill-nya, serta Basic Attack-nya juga memberikan efek Overload yang men-trigger Spell Vamp. Oleh karena itu, sustain awalnya lebih baik menggunakan Spell Vamp ketimbang Life Steal. Ini menjadi pertimbangan mengapa saya menggunakan emblem Tank ketimbang Emblem Fighter, karena Emblem Fighter hanya memberikan Life Steal, sedangkan Emblem Tank memberikan HP dan Hybrid Defense yang lebih kepakai.
Festival of Blood (Ability) : 6% Spell Vamp + 0.5% per Kill/Assist (max 12 kali)
Ice Queen Wand : 10% Spell Vamp

Pada mid dan late game, Life steal juga bisa dipakai, terutama jika menggunakan build Feather of Heaven (bisa ditambah Concentrated Energy dan Inspire)

Kelemahan Edith
1) Edith kuatnya jika nge-stack Physical & Magic Defense
Thunderbelt : 15 Phy + 15 Mag, bisa tambah stack Hybrid Defense (20- sampai 30- an rata-rata)
Dipakai untuk item pertama agar stacknya bisa menggulung lebih awal.
Item core karena mengatasi poin 2) sekaligus memberikan dmg tambahan (50 + Total Defense ketika Basic Attack)
Dominance Ice : 40 Phy + 40 Magic, dan memberikan tambahan (8 Phy + 8 Mag) untuk setiap lawan di dekatnya. Jadinya bisa berpotensi untuk memberikan 160 Total Defense
Blade Armor : + 80 Phy, kadang dipakai dibandingkan Radiant Armor yang berpotensi memberikan 140 Mag, untuk meng-counter mobility (karena memberikan efek slow) dan dmg hero Marksman late game. 

2) Edith jalannya lama
Rapid Boot : Sangat membantu mobilitas, serta mengurangi efek slow. Namun jika hero banyak CC, ganti menggunakan Tough Boots untuk CC Duration Reduction
Thunderbelt : + 20 Movement Speed, sekaligus memberikan efek slow
Dominance Ice : +5% Movement Speed
Starlium Scythe : Efek slow 15%
Agility (Ability) : 4% Movement Speed
Ice Queen Wand : + 7 Movement Speed, sekaligus memberikan efek slow

Tambahan
Concussive Blast (Ability) dipakai untuk mempercepat laning Edith, agar bisa membantu team fight yang lain lebih cepat. Bawalah ia War sering-sering!!!!
Flicker dipakai dibandingkan Inspire karena saya tidak suka lawan kabur-kaburan.

113.title:
This build works really well for the EXP lane because it gives a solid balance of damage and sustain, which helps a lot with my rotations when I’m using Yin. I usually start by cutting the lane, especially if I’m up against sustain-heavy fighters like Yu Zhong, Ruby, or Phoveus. Doing this lets me control the wave early and rotate faster to help my team.

If I’m facing squishier fighters like Guinevere or Zilong, I switch to the Fighter Emblem for higher damage. It’s a more aggressive setup, but it still gives enough sustain to stay in fights.

For items, I like to build Fury Hammer first so I can get early penetration. After that, I go for Sea Halberd since it’s a big help for Yin, especially against enemies with sustain.

As for spells, Petrify is a must-have because it helps extend your crowd control and makes it easier to secure kills. But depending on your playstyle or role, you can also use Flicker, Execute, or even Retribution if you’re going jungle.

Overall, this setup keeps Yin flexible and effective, no matter the matchup.
